name: Singapore Chilli Crab
recipeInstructions:
1. This Singapore dish is a classic and, like all classics, it is superb.
Serve it with plain rice.
2. Boil a large pot of water and drop in 4 green blue swimmers.
Boil them rapidly for 4 minutes then tip the contents of the pot into a colander to drain.
When cool enough to handle, chop crabs into quarters with a cleaver or large knife,
leaving legs attached to body.
3. With a hammer, gently crack the claws and areas of harder shell.
4. Put 3 teaspoons tamarind pulp to soak in 3/4 cup boiling water.
Finely chop 2 onions, grate 5 cm piece fresh ginger, finely slice 2-3 birdseye chillies (or to taste)
and chop 3 green (spring) onions.
5. Mix 2 teaspoons cornflour with a little water in a small bowl.
6. In a large wok or pan, heat 3 tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat.
Stir-fry onion, ginger, and 2/3 of the chopped chillies for a couple of minutes until fragrant.
7. Add crab pieces and strained tamarind water to the wok.
Lower the heat to medium-low and simmer for 3-4 minutes, stirring occasionally.
8. Lift the crab out onto a serving dish using tongs or a slotted spoon.
9. In the same wok, add 3 teaspoons sugar and 2 teaspoons tomato paste.
Stir until well combined.
10. Add the dissolved cornflour mixture to the saucepan and stir again until thickened.
Pour the sauce over the crab, garnish with the remaining chillies and green onions,
and serve immediately.
